binaries.title: csc.sys - No security-relevant change: WIL feature-staging gate around a NULL check in CscDclMRxTransition (dclmrx.c) graduated to unconditional

changed_functions.name: CscDclMRxTransition (0x1C005CBA4)
key_changes: REMOVED: Two calls to EvaluateCurrentState (0x1C0007440) at 0x1C005CC19 (prologue) and 0x1C005D181 (epilogue) in the unpatched binary. EvaluateCurrentState is a WIL feature-staging helper: it calls EvaluateFeature (0x1C00074EC) and returns (g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_cachedstate != 1). EvaluateFeature stores EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry(...)+1 into the cached state, so the value is 2 when the feature resolves to enabled and 1 when it resolves to disabled.

CHANGED (prologue guard): unpatched error-exit test is 'if ((a2-1) > 2 || EvaluateCurrentState() && v8 == 0)'. The v8==NULL check is AND-combined with the feature evaluation, so it is skipped only when EvaluateCurrentState() is false (cached-state == 1, i.e. the feature is disabled). Patched build drops the feature gate to 'if ((a2-1) > 2 || v8 == 0)', making the NULL check unconditional.

CHANGED (epilogue guard): unpatched 'if (!EvaluateCurrentState() || v8 != 0) CscFlushLViewWasPreviouslyOffline(v8);' becomes patched 'if (v8 != 0) CscFlushLViewWasPreviouslyOffline(v8);' (asm: test rdi,rdi; jz; call at 0x1C005D178). Same feature gate removed.

The pointer is v8 (register rdi), a logical-view (LView) pointer loaded as a1->Header.FilterContexts.Flink then Flink[7].Flink (asm: mov rax,[rcx+0x38]; mov rdi,[rax+0x70]). Its consumers are CscAcquireLViewLock (0x1C000BD78), CscLViewIsRootGhosted (0x1C00621B4) and CscReleaseLViewLock (0x1C000BEEC).

All other diff hunks are cosmetic: WPP trace-GUID symbol renames (WPP_9227ea88... -> WPP_9a6a71d2...), differing __LINE__ constants (0x2FB->0x2F6, 0x305->0x300) in RxpTrackReference/RxpTrackDereference, and branch/label target shifts caused by the removed call/test/jz sequences. No other behavioral change.
changed_functions.security_notes: Defense-in-depth / WIL feature-staging graduation, not a delivered security fix. CscDclMRxTransition (0x1C005CBA4) is the transition handler in csc.sys (Windows Offline Files / Client-Side Caching mini-redirector, source base\fs\remotefs\rdr\csc\driver\dclmrx.c). It loads a logical-view pointer v8 = a1->Header.FilterContexts.Flink[7].Flink (asm rdi = *(*(rcx+0x38)+0x70)), which can legitimately be NULL. In the unpatched build the NULL check on v8 is AND-gated behind a WIL feature evaluation (EvaluateCurrentState, 0x1C0007440). Because EvaluateFeature encodes disabled=1 / enabled=2 and EvaluateCurrentState returns (cachedstate != 1), the check is skipped ONLY when the feature is in the disabled state. That state is set by Microsoft's feature-flighting / registry-override infrastructure, not by an attacker, and is not the default. In the default (feature-enabled) configuration the NULL check is active in BOTH builds, so there is no attacker-reachable behavioral difference. The patch removes the feature gate at both guard sites, baking the check in permanently. Even in the disabled-feature state the only possible outcome is a kernel NULL-pointer dereference bug check (local DoS); the callees read fixed small offsets (+0x30, +0x38), NULL-page mapping is blocked on modern Windows, and there is no controlled-data, info-leak, or code-execution primitive. Weakness class touched: CWE-476 (NULL pointer dereference).

security_findings.entry_point: FSCTL dispatch on the CSC driver device; transition sub-operation with a2 in {1,2,3}
call_chain: DeviceIoControl / NtDeviceIoControlFile on CSC device (FSCTL dispatch)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Defense-in-depth hardening: a WIL feature-staging gate around a NULL check (CWE-476 class) is graduated to unconditional. Not attacker-reachable in the default configuration.
security_findings.description: CscDclMRxTransition (0x1C005CBA4) is the transition handler in csc.sys (Windows Offline Files mini-redirector). It loads a logical-view pointer v8 = a1->Header.FilterContexts.Flink[7].Flink (asm: rax = *(rcx+0x38); rdi = *(rax+0x70)), which can be NULL. In the unpatched build the prologue guard is 'if ((a2-1) > 2 || EvaluateCurrentState() && v8 == 0)'. EvaluateCurrentState (0x1C0007440) is a WIL feature-staging helper that returns (g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_cachedstate != 1); EvaluateFeature (0x1C00074EC) stores enabled=2 / disabled=1 into the cached state. Because the v8==0 term is AND-combined with EvaluateCurrentState(), the NULL check is skipped ONLY when the feature is disabled (cached-state == 1). That is a vendor/cloud-flighting-controlled state, not the default and not attacker-controlled; with the feature enabled the NULL check is active in both builds. The patch removes the EvaluateCurrentState gate at both the prologue and a matching epilogue guard, making both v8 NULL checks unconditional. This is a feature-staging graduation (defense-in-depth), not a fix for an attacker-reachable vulnerability.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: The skip path exists only when ALL of the following hold, none of which is the default or attacker-controlled: (1) the WIL feature g_Feature_4283659579_58821281 is in the disabled state (cached-state == 1) so EvaluateCurrentState() returns 0; (2) a handle to the CSC device is obtained and the transition sub-operation is issued with a2 in {1,2,3}; (3) the logical-view pointer v8 = *(*(RX_CONTEXT+0x38)+0x70) is NULL (associated FCB/context has no logical view allocated for the object being transitioned) - the exact state producing this was not confirmed from the binaries. In the default (feature-enabled) configuration the NULL check is active in both builds and no crash occurs.
security_findings.primitive: None beyond, at most, a local kernel NULL-pointer dereference bug check (denial of service) confined to the non-default feature-disabled state. The callees read fixed small offsets (+0x30, +0x38); NULL-page mapping is blocked on modern Windows, so there is no controlled data at address 0, no info leak, and no code-execution primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
; === UNPATCHED CscDclMRxTransition (0x1C005CBA4) -- prologue guard ===
  0x1C005CBFF: lea     ecx, [r13-1]              ; ecx = a2 - 1  (r13d = a2)
  0x1C005CC0A: cmp     ecx, 2
  0x1C005CC0D: jbe     loc_1C005CC19             ; a2 in {1,2,3}
  0x1C005CC0F: mov     esi, 0C000000Dh          ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
  0x1C005CC14: jmp     loc_1C005D174            ; error exit
  0x1C005CC19: call    EvaluateCurrentState      ; WIL feature evaluation (0x1C0007440)
  0x1C005CC1E: test    eax, eax                  ; eax==0 only when feature disabled (cachedstate==1)
  0x1C005CC20: jz      loc_1C005CC27            ; feature disabled -> SKIP the NULL check
  0x1C005CC22: test    rdi, rdi                  ; NULL check on v8, taken when feature enabled
  0x1C005CC25: jz      loc_1C005CC0F            ; NULL -> error exit
  0x1C005CC27: mov     rcx, rdi                  ; rdi (v8) NULL here only if feature disabled
  0x1C005CC2A: call    CscAcquireLViewLock       ; 0x1C000BD78
  0x1C005CC2F: mov     rcx, rdi
  0x1C005CC32: call    CscLViewIsRootGhosted     ; 0x1C00621B4

; === UNPATCHED -- epilogue guard (second gated NULL check) ===
  0x1C005D181: call    EvaluateCurrentState
  0x1C005D186: test    eax, eax
  0x1C005D188: jz      loc_1C005D18F            ; feature disabled -> skip NULL check
  0x1C005D18A: test    rdi, rdi
  0x1C005D18D: jz      loc_1C005D197
  0x1C005D18F: mov     rcx, rdi
  0x1C005D192: call    CscFlushLViewWasPreviouslyOffline  ; 0x1C0061EEC

; === PATCHED CscDclMRxTransition (0x1C005CBA4) -- prologue guard ===
  0x1C005CC0D: jbe     loc_1C005CC19
  0x1C005CC0F: mov     esi, 0C000000Dh          ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
  0x1C005CC14: jmp     loc_1C005D16B
  0x1C005CC19: test    rdi, rdi                  ; UNCONDITIONAL NULL check (feature gate removed)
  0x1C005CC1C: jz      loc_1C005CC0F            ; NULL -> error exit
  0x1C005CC1E: mov     rcx, rdi
  0x1C005CC21: call    CscAcquireLViewLock

; === PATCHED -- epilogue guard (unconditional) ===
  0x1C005D178: test    rdi, rdi
  0x1C005D17B: jz      loc_1C005D185
  0x1C005D17D: mov     rcx, rdi
  0x1C005D180: call    CscFlushLViewWasPreviouslyOffline

; EvaluateCurrentState (0x1C0007440) -- WIL feature helper, calls removed by patch:
  0x1C0007444: lea     rcx, g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_FeatureDescriptorDetails
  0x1C000744B: call    EvaluateFeature           ; 0x1C00074EC
  0x1C0007450: mov     rax, cs:g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_FeatureDescriptorDetails
  0x1C0007457: mov     ecx, [rax]                ; cached state
  0x1C000745B: cmp     ecx, 1                    ; == 1 means feature disabled
  0x1C000745E: setnz   al                        ; return (cachedstate != 1)
  0x1C0007465: retn

breakpoints.why: Entry to the transition handler. rcx = a1 (RX_CONTEXT/FCB), edx = a2 (sub-operation, must be 1-3). v8 (rdi) is loaded as *(*(rcx+0x38)+0x70) - the logical-view pointer that may be NULL.

WIL feature-state helper. Return value in eax: 0 means g_Feature_...cachedstate == 1 (feature disabled), which is the state where the v8 NULL check is skipped. With the feature enabled it returns non-zero and the check runs, matching the patched build.
call EvaluateCurrentState in the prologue guard. After it returns, if eax==0 (feature disabled) the jz at 0x1C005CC20 skips the test rdi,rdi NULL check.
call CscAcquireLViewLock (0x1C000BD78) with rcx = rdi = v8. NULL here is reachable only when the feature is disabled and v8 == NULL.

watch: rdi register -- holds v8, the logical-view pointer (*(*(rcx+0x38)+0x70))
eax after call to EvaluateCurrentState -- 0 means feature disabled (skip path); non-zero means NULL check active (same as patched)
edx (a2) -- must be 1, 2, or 3 so that (a2-1) <= 2
rcx (a1) -- RX_CONTEXT/FCB; *(rcx+0x38) is dereferenced to get rax, then *(rax+0x70) yields rdi (v8)
g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_cachedstate -- WIL feature cached state: 1 = disabled (skip path), 2 = enabled (check active)
key_offsets: 0x1C005CC19: call EvaluateCurrentState -- the removed feature evaluation (prologue)
0x1C005CC1E-0x1C005CC20: test eax,eax; jz 0x1C005CC27 -- branch that skips the NULL check when the feature is disabled
0x1C005CC22: test rdi,rdi -- the gated NULL check
0x1C005CC27-0x1C005CC2A: mov rcx,rdi; call CscAcquireLViewLock -- first use of v8
0x1C005D181: call EvaluateCurrentState -- second occurrence of the removed feature gate (epilogue)
g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_cachedstate: WIL feature cached state; 1 = disabled -> skip path
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: The behavioral difference is observable only in the non-default feature-disabled state: (1) ensure g_Feature_4283659579_58821281 resolves to disabled (cached-state == 1) so EvaluateCurrentState() returns 0; (2) open a handle to the CSC device and issue the transition FSCTL sub-operation with a2 in {1,2,3}; (3) drive the associated FCB/context so the logical-view pointer (*(RX_CONTEXT+0x38)->0x70) is NULL. In that combination the unpatched handler skips the NULL check and faults at CscAcquireLViewLock(v8). With the feature enabled, the unpatched build behaves like the patched build and returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the feature-disabled state only: kernel bug check (e.g. PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA / access violation) referencing address 0x0 or a small offset (+0x30, +0x38) inside CscAcquireLViewLock/CscLViewIsRootGhosted, called from CscDclMRxTransition. In the default (feature-enabled) configuration, or in the patched build, the handler returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xC000000D) instead.
debugging_aids.notes: The driver is csc.sys (Windows Offline Files / Client-Side Caching mini-redirector), source base\fs\remotefs\rdr\csc\driver\dclmrx.c, built on the RDBSS framework. v8 is a logical-view (LView) pointer; it is NULL when no logical view is allocated for the object being transitioned. EvaluateCurrentState (0x1C0007440) is a WIL feature-staging helper (EvaluateFeature + g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_cachedstate != 1), not a driver-active flag. The unpatched build gated the defensive NULL check behind this feature so it could be turned off via flighting; the patch graduates the check to unconditional. This is defense-in-depth / servicing churn, not a fix for an attacker-reachable vulnerability. On modern Windows the NULL page cannot be user-mapped, so even the disabled-state worst case is a local DoS.

summary: The only functional change is in CscDclMRxTransition (0x1C005CBA4), the transition handler of csc.sys (Windows Offline Files, source dclmrx.c). In the unpatched build a NULL check on the logical-view pointer v8 is AND-gated behind a WIL feature-staging evaluation: the prologue guard is 'if ((a2-1) > 2 || EvaluateCurrentState() && v8 == 0)'. EvaluateCurrentState (0x1C0007440) returns (g_Feature_4283659579_58821281_cachedstate != 1), and EvaluateFeature (0x1C00074EC) encodes disabled=1 / enabled=2, so the NULL check is skipped only when the feature is disabled - a vendor/cloud-flighting-controlled state, not the default and not attacker-controlled. With the feature enabled the NULL check is active in both builds. The patch removes the EvaluateCurrentState gate at the prologue and a matching epilogue guard, making both v8 NULL checks unconditional. This is a WIL feature-staging graduation (defense-in-depth), not a fix for an attacker-reachable vulnerability. Its maximum theoretical impact, and only in the non-default feature-disabled state, is a local kernel NULL-pointer dereference bug check (DoS); there is no controlled-data, info-leak, or code-execution primitive. Verdict: No security-relevant change. Weakness class touched: CWE-476.
